sourceMovie.title: The School for Good and Evil
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The School for Good and Evil (2022) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul Feig's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Last Action Hero
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The School for Good and Evil clicked for you, Last Action Hero (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The School for Good and Evil clicked for you, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The School for Good and Evil?
faq.answer: Try Last Action Hero, Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone, Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban—each shares DNA with The School for Good and Evil while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The School for Good and Evil?
faq.answer: The School for Good and Evil (2022) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The School for Good and Evil worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
